div1.entryFree:
Front, vb. 1) trans. a) to face, to oppose, to defy, to meet: what well-appointed leader --s us here? H4B IV, 1, 25. death doth f. thee with apparent spoil, H6A IV, 2, 26. f. him to his face, H6B V, 1, 86. Cor. V, 2, 44. Ant. I, 4, 79. Absol.: towards --ing peril, H4B IV, 4, 66.
b) to attack: f. her, board her, Tw. I, 3, 59. you four shall f. them in the narrow lane, H4A II, 2, 62. those wars which --ed mine own peace, Ant. II, 2, 61.
c) to stand over against: a gate of steel --ing the sun, Troil. III, 3, 122.
d) to form an outwork for, to fortify: yonder walls that f. your town, Troil. IV, 5, 219.
2) intr. to march in the front or first rank: and f. but in that file where others tell steps with me, H8 I, 2, 42.
